title: Material Procedure Change Invalidates Prior Consent
scenario: A patient consents to a procedure, but a material procedural change occurs after consent and before execution.
initial_state: Consent was valid for the original procedure plan.
state_transition: A material change alters the risk, method, or scope of the procedure.
execution_boundary: The changed procedure is evaluated before execution.
constitutional_evaluation: Consent continuity requires that consent remain informed and applicable to the procedure actually being executed.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because the material change breaks continuity between informed consent and execution.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when material changes invalidate the original consent basis.
